codeReview.summary: Feature implementation covers the requested launcher flows (create/switch/rename), expected negative boundaries for blank/whitespace/duplicate/missing/same-name branches, and discoverability/no-dead-end behavior evidence. One non-blocking multi-client reliability issue was found in switch targeting logic.

issues.description: Switch flow picks the first client attached to the current session (`first_client_for_session`) and then calls `switch-client -c <tty>`, which can switch the wrong client when multiple clients are attached to the same workspace. The invoking client's tty should be targeted instead.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tmux-plugin-worker` skill lacks explicit TERM compatibility guidance for tuistory/tmux interactive verification, even though workers can hit terminal-type failures in practice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `skillFeedback.suggestedChanges` requests documenting `TERM=screen-256color` for tuistory+tmux, while this guidance currently exists in `.factory/library/user-testing.md` but not in `.factory/skills/tmux-plugin-worker/SKILL.md`.

summary: Reviewed mission artifacts, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit c1bc818 diff for workspace-launcher-create-switch-rename. Result is PASS with one non-blocking reliability issue (multi-client switch targeting) and one shared-state skill-documentation observation.
